text.tspan: SPACE-BASED ARCHITECTURE: scale by removing the database bottleneck.
Keep ALL active state in a distributed in-memory grid ('the space');
let many stateless processing units read/write it; persist asynchronously.
TRADITIONAL N-TIER (the problem)
Web tier (scales out) u2192 App tier (scales out)
u2192 DB tier (bottleneck; vertical-scale until very expensive)
Under burst: DB saturates; app blocks on DB; latency explodes.
WHEN IT WINS
u2022 sustained high concurrency where DB is the bottleneck
u2022 burst patterns (flash sales, ticket releases, market open)
u2022 low-latency requirements that traditional DB can't hit
u2022 stateful sessions that don't fit in stateless tier
CANONICAL USES
u2022 stock trading / market-making systems
u2022 airline reservations / GDS systems
u2022 online betting at peak
u2022 risk calculation grids
u2022 large multiplayer games (region servers)
TRADE-OFFS
u2022 in-memory means SIZE LIMITS (TBs, not PBs)
u2022 consistency depends on grid implementation
u2022 operational complexity is HIGH
u2022 framework lock-in (Hazelcast/Ignite/Coherence)
u2022 NOT for ad-hoc OLAP, document storage, big data
PROCESSING UNITS (many, identical, ephemeral)
each holds part of the data in-memory (the 'space')
business logic runs on the same node as the data
data replicated across PUs for HA
PUs can come & go; data rebalances via [consistent hashing](../data/consistent-hashing.md)
DATA GRID (the SPACE)
Hazelcast / Apache Ignite / GridGain / Coherence / GemFire / Memcached
shared in-memory key-value tuple space
topologies: partitioned + replicated
PUs read/write locally most of the time
ASYNC PERSISTENCE
data writer process drains updates from space u2192 RDBMS / object store
database is no longer in the hot path
loss tolerance: recover from log replay
MESSAGING GRID + PROCESSING GRID + DEPLOYMENT GRID
request router u2192 PU with the right data
workflow orchestration across PUs
manage PU lifecycle, scaling, failover
